message:
fix(rate_limit_tracker): clamp parsed Retry-After at MAX_COOLDOWN_SECS to prevent Instant + Duration overflow (ft-7ojbj)

parse_retry_after() previously returned whatever u64 the header text
parsed into, wrapped in Duration::from_secs(). A plain numeric value of
u64::MAX (18_446_744_073_709_551_615) parses cleanly, produces
Duration::from_secs(u64::MAX) — roughly 584 billion years — and then
blows up inside PaneRateLimitState::record_event when the tracker
computes `event.detected_at + event.cooldown`. `Instant + Duration`
panics on overflow (documented), so the very first record_at() on that
pane would crash the whole tracker thread.

Real-world Retry-After values from providers we care about (OpenAI,
Anthropic, Google) top out around an hour. Anything beyond 24h is a
signal to bail the whole pane rather than try to hold the state
forever — the clamp keeps the tracker operational and lets the pane's
normal quota-exhaustion logic pick up from there.

## The fix: MAX_COOLDOWN_SECS = 24h, clamped at the parser

Introduce `const MAX_COOLDOWN_SECS: u64 = 24 * 60 * 60` at the top of
the module with a comment-block explaining both the overflow-panic
attack surface and the rationale for 24h specifically. Apply `.min()`
clamps inside parse_retry_after at both entry points:

  - plain-number path (secs): `Duration::from_secs(secs.min(MAX_COOLDOWN_SECS))`
  - "N unit" path (secs after checked_mul): same clamp

Doc comments on parse_retry_after describe the clamp so future readers
don't mistake it for a filter that drops hostile values — it does NOT
drop them, it re-fits them into a safe Duration so the tracker never
observes a number that will panic the downstream Instant arithmetic.

## Regression tests (3 new)

  - parse_retry_after_plain_u64_max_is_clamped:
    feed format!("{}", u64::MAX); assert parsed ==
    Duration::from_secs(MAX_COOLDOWN_SECS). Before the clamp, the
    parser returned Duration::from_secs(u64::MAX) unclamped.

  - parse_retry_after_huge_hours_is_clamped:
    feed "1000000 hours" (in u64 range; pre-clamp this was a valid
    ~114-year duration, still absurd). Assert parsed <=
    Duration::from_secs(MAX_COOLDOWN_SECS).

  - record_at_with_u64_max_retry_after_does_not_panic:
    end-to-end — construct a fresh tracker, record_at with the
    u64::MAX retry-after event, and assert no panic. Before the
    clamp, this panicked at the first Instant + cooldown inside
    record_event.

Tracks ft-7ojbj (parse_retry_after u64::MAX overflow panic).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
